# diagramkit

> Diagram rendering CLI & library. Converts .mermaid, .excalidraw, .drawio, and Graphviz .dot/.gv/.graphviz files to SVG/PNG/JPEG/WebP/AVIF with automatic light/dark mode support.

## Quick Start

```bash
npm add diagramkit
npx diagramkit warmup
npx diagramkit render .
```

## Supported Formats

Input: .mermaid (.mmd, .mmdc), .excalidraw, .drawio (.drawio.xml, .dio), .dot (.gv, .graphviz)
Output: SVG (default), PNG, JPEG, WebP, AVIF

Prefer SVG unless the destination explicitly needs raster (email, Confluence).

## CLI

```
diagramkit render <file-or-dir> [options]            Render diagram file(s) to images
diagramkit <file-or-dir> [options]                   Alias for "diagramkit render"
diagramkit validate <file-or-dir> [--recursive]      Validate generated SVGs (img-tag compatibility)
                                  [--json]
diagramkit warmup                                    Pre-install Playwright chromium
diagramkit doctor [--json]                           Validate runtime dependencies
diagramkit init [--ts] [--yes]                       Create config file
diagramkit                                           Interactive picker (TTY only)
diagramkit --interactive / -i                        Force interactive wizard (TTY)
diagramkit --no-interactive / --yes / -y             Disable interactive wizard (CI / agents)
diagramkit --version / --help / --agent-help

Project skills are NOT installed by the diagramkit CLI. Use the standalone
"skills" CLI (https://github.com/vercel-labs/skills) so any agent
(Claude/Cursor/Codex/Continue/OpenCode/...) gets diagramkit-* skills:

  npx skills add sujeet-pro/diagramkit              # all skills
  npx skills add sujeet-pro/diagramkit -a cursor    # target specific agent
  npx skills add sujeet-pro/diagramkit \
    -s diagramkit-setup -s diagramkit-mermaid       # specific skills only
  npx skills update sujeet-pro/diagramkit           # refresh later
```

### Render Options

```
--format <formats>       Output formats, comma-separated (default: svg)
--theme <light|dark|both> Theme variants (default: both)
--scale <number>         Scale for raster output (default: 2)
--quality <number>       JPEG/WebP/AVIF quality 1-100 (default: 90)
--force                  Re-render all, ignore manifest
--watch                  Watch for changes and re-render
--no-contrast            Disable dark SVG contrast optimization
--type <type>            Filter: mermaid | excalidraw | drawio | graphviz
--output <dir>           Custom output directory
--config <path>          Path to config file (skip auto-discovery)
--output-dir <name>      Output folder name (default: .diagramkit)
--manifest-file <name>   Manifest filename (default: manifest.json)
--no-manifest            Disable manifest tracking
--same-folder            Output next to source files
--output-prefix <string> Prefix for output filenames (default: '')
--output-suffix <string> Suffix for output filenames (default: '')
--dry-run                Preview what would render
--plan                   Show stale plan with reasons (directory mode)
--quiet                  Suppress info output
--json                   Machine-readable JSON output
--log-level <level>      Logging verbosity (silent/error/warn/info/verbose)
--strict-config          Fail on config warnings/invalid values
--strict                 Exit non-zero on any single render failure
--max-type-lanes <1-4>   Limit concurrent engine lanes
--interactive / -i       Force the interactive wizard (TTY only)
--no-interactive         Disable the interactive wizard (CI / agents)
--yes / -y               Alias for --no-interactive (accept defaults)
```

### Validate

```bash
diagramkit validate .diagramkit/                # all SVGs in a folder
diagramkit validate .diagramkit/ --recursive    # recurse into subfolders
diagramkit validate output.svg                  # single file
diagramkit validate . --recursive --json        # JSON output for CI
```

The `validate` command checks each `.svg` for missing `xmlns`, missing dimensions,
embedded `<script>`, broken `<foreignObject>`, and external resource references —
issues that silently degrade in `<img>`-based markdown embeds.

### Examples

```bash
diagramkit render .                                  # All diagrams in cwd
diagramkit render flow.mermaid                       # Single file
diagramkit render . --format svg,png                 # Multi-format
diagramkit render ./docs --format png --scale 3      # High-res PNGs
diagramkit render . --watch                          # Watch mode
diagramkit render . --output ./build/images          # Custom output dir
diagramkit render . --type mermaid --force           # Force mermaid only
diagramkit render . --config ./custom.config.json5   # Explicit config
```

## Output Convention

Rendered images go to `.diagramkit/` next to source files:

```
docs/
  architecture.mermaid
  .diagramkit/
    architecture-light.svg
    architecture-dark.svg
    manifest.json
```

## Configuration

Config file: `diagramkit.config.json5` or `diagramkit.config.ts`
Global: `~/.config/diagramkit/config.json5`
Merge order: defaults → global → env vars (DIAGRAMKIT_*) → local → CLI flags

Key options: outputDir, manifestFile, useManifest, sameFolder, defaultFormats, defaultTheme, outputPrefix, outputSuffix, extensionMap, overrides (per-file settings).

JSON Schema (for IDE autocomplete in `diagramkit.config.json5` / `.json`):

```json5
{
  $schema: './node_modules/diagramkit/schemas/diagramkit-config.v1.json',
  defaultFormats: ['svg'],
  defaultTheme: 'both',
}
```

`diagramkit init` writes the `$schema` reference for you. The schema also ships at
`diagramkit/schemas/diagramkit-config.v1.json` as a package export.

Per-file overrides example:
```json5
{
  overrides: {
    "hero.mermaid": { formats: ["svg", "png"], scale: 3 },
    "docs/*.excalidraw": { theme: "light" },
  },
}
```

## Dependencies

All rendering engines (mermaid, excalidraw, draw.io, graphviz) are bundled.
Optional: `sharp` (for PNG/JPEG/WebP/AVIF raster output)

## Agent Files

### AI Guidelines (recommended)

The `ai-guidelines/` folder in the npm package provides structured agent guidance:

- `ai-guidelines/usage.md` - primary agent instructions (start here)
- `ai-guidelines/diagram-authoring.md` - exhaustive diagram authoring guide (all engines, colors, theming)
- `llms.txt` - compact CLI reference
- `llms-full.txt` - full CLI + API reference

### Root LLM Files

- `llms.txt` - practical defaults and CLI usage
- `llms-full.txt` - complete CLI + API + type reference

Use `diagramkit --agent-help` when you want the full reference in one command output.

## Repo Bootstrap With Agents

After installing the package, agents should read in this order:

1. `node_modules/diagramkit/REFERENCE.md` — landing page (always read first; reflects the exact installed version)
2. `node_modules/diagramkit/ai-guidelines/usage.md` — primary agent setup instructions
3. `node_modules/diagramkit/ai-guidelines/diagram-authoring.md` — per-engine authoring rules

Recommended repo bootstrap steps:

- `npm add diagramkit` (always prefer the local install over a global one)
- add `"render:diagrams": "diagramkit render ."` to `package.json`
- create `diagramkit.config.json5` only when the repository needs non-default behavior (use `npx diagramkit init --yes` to wire the JSON Schema)
- install diagramkit project skills via the standalone `skills` CLI so any agent works: `npx skills add sujeet-pro/diagramkit`
- run `npx diagramkit warmup` unless the repository is Graphviz-only

### Copy-paste agent prompt (bootstrap)

```text
Set up diagramkit in this repo:
1. npm add diagramkit
2. Read node_modules/diagramkit/REFERENCE.md (use the LOCAL install, not a
   globally installed CLI).
3. npx diagramkit warmup        # skip if Graphviz-only
4. npx diagramkit init --yes    # only if non-default behavior is needed
5. Add "render:diagrams": "diagramkit render ." to package.json
6. npx skills add sujeet-pro/diagramkit   # installs diagramkit-* agent skills
7. npx diagramkit render .
```

### Copy-paste agent prompt (generate a diagram + raster exports)

```text
Use the diagramkit-* skills in this repo (installed via
`npx skills add sujeet-pro/diagramkit`) to create a [TOPIC] diagram. Read
node_modules/diagramkit/REFERENCE.md first to anchor on the locally installed
diagramkit version. Use diagramkit-auto to choose the engine, then the
matching engine skill. Save the source under `diagrams/`. Render to multiple
formats:
  npx diagramkit render diagrams/<file> --format svg,png,webp --scale 2
Embed it in the relevant markdown using the <picture> pattern.
```

## Programmatic API

```ts
import { render, renderFile, renderAll, watchDiagrams, loadConfig, warmup, dispose } from 'diagramkit'
```

`--json` returns a versioned envelope (`schemaVersion: 1`) with nested `result`.
JSON schemas (exported from the npm package):
- `diagramkit/schemas/diagramkit-cli-render.v1.json` — CLI render JSON output envelope
- `diagramkit/schemas/diagramkit-config.v1.json` — `diagramkit.config.{json5,json}` config file

Exit codes: `0` = success, `1` = error (render failure, unknown command, etc.).
Use `--quiet --json` together for clean JSON on stdout (suppresses log noise).
